_comment: Example for create-broadcast.js — the 3-step broadcast flow
_note: create-broadcast.js orchestrates all 3 steps. Use this as a reference for the flags it accepts.

smart_delivery._description: Batched broadcast with auto-pacing based on read rate

regular_delivery_with_variables._description: Fixed-batch broadcast with per-recipient body variables in the CSV
regular_delivery_with_variables._note: body1, body2, body3 columns in the CSV map to {{1}}, {{2}}, {{3}} in the template body per recipient

delivery_modes.smart: Adaptive batching — waits for read_rate % of a batch to be read before sending the next. Safest for large lists.
delivery_modes.regular: Fixed-size batches at a constant pace. Predictable but no read-rate feedback loop.
delivery_modes.risk: No batching — all messages sent at once. Only for very small urgent lists. Can trigger Meta spam detection.

csv_rules: phone_number column must NOT include + prefix — use 14155550101, not +14155550101
Variable columns (body1, body2, body3) map to {{1}}, {{2}}, {{3}} in the template
media column: public URL for per-recipient media (image/document/video templates)
button_dynamic_url_value column: per-recipient URL suffix for CTA buttons
Xano deduplicates phone numbers — duplicates in CSV are silently dropped
Re-uploading CSV to same broadcast_identifier deletes the previous recipient list first
